description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone, reminiscent of early 20th-century designs, featuring a cylindrical metal body mounted on ornate, curved legs. The handset is suspended on a horizontal cradle at the top, with a coiled cord connecting it to the main body. The base is robust and metallic, with visible gears and a hand crank on one side, suggesting a manual operation mechanism. Decorative elements, such as gold trim and engraved text reading 'Stockholms Telefon,' add to its historical character. The rotary dial is absent, indicating that calls may have been initiated by turning the crank. The overall construction is primarily metal, with some wooden and plastic components, and the finish is glossy with a slightly reflective surface.
description: A vintage, ornate corded telephone with a cylindrical metal body, hand crank, and suspended handset on a cradle, featuring decorative gold trim and a coiled cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical body, hand crank on the left, suspended handset, and decorative gold trim on the legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the hand crank, gear mechanism, and the coiled cord draped to the right.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cylindrical body, the coiled cord, and the symmetrical legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the gear mechanism, engraved text 'Stockholms Telefon,' and the handset cradle.
